message:
feat(patterns): add fallback SQL DELETE detection for compound commands

When a segment of a compound command is not matched by existing pattern
rules, apply a fallback check for SQL DELETE statements:

- DELETE FROM without WHERE clause -> RiskTierCritical
  (matched as "fallback_sql_delete_no_where")
- DELETE FROM with WHERE clause -> RiskTierDangerous
  (matched as "fallback_sql_delete_with_where")

This closes a gap where piped or chained SQL DELETE commands could slip
through unclassified if no explicit pattern existed for the specific
command wrapper (e.g., `echo "DELETE FROM users" | psql`).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
